name: Andy Kirk
description: American jazz saxophonist and tubist, bandleader (1898-1992)
abstract: Andrew Dewey Kirk (May 28, 1898 – December 11, 1992) was an American jazz bandleader and saxophonist who led the Twelve Clouds of Joy, a band popular during the swing era.

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Alden
about_quotes.evidence: Floyd Smith made a record with Andy Kirk’s band called “Floyd’s Guitar Blues,”
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Alden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Howard McGhee
about_quotes.evidence: I put it in Kirk's band... I really enjoyed being with Kirk... I just took the book and I put the arrangement in Andy's band, and Andy featured me on it.

about_quotes.speaker: Howard McGhee

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Tate
about_quotes.evidence: my first professional band that I worked with was with a band called T. Holders Twelve Clouds of Joy, who later became Andy Kirk’s Twelve Clouds of Joy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy Tate interviewed by Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Howard McGhee

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: I was engaged by Andy Kirk to replace a trumpeter named Clarence Trice... I stayed with the orchestra for four months
